scanner: manual code review + grep/pattern analysis + config inspection + external .env audit

vulnerabilities.description: Hardcoded DeepSeek API key. Live credential in plaintext config. Used by fast_evaluation and deep_evaluation backends for LLM calls.
vulnerabilities.impact: Full API access to DeepSeek LLM services. Attacker can consume credits, access model outputs, and exfiltrate financial analysis data.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Rotate key immediately via DeepSeek dashboard. Replace with empty string placeholder. Load from DEEPSEEK_API_KEY environment variable at runtime.

vulnerabilities.description: Hardcoded Google API key. Duplicated at lines 9 (common) and 18 (google section). Used by TradingAgents news fetching.
vulnerabilities.impact: Unauthorized access to Google Cloud APIs. Attacker can consume API quota and access associated Google Cloud resources.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Rotate key in Google Cloud Console. Remove from both config locations. Use GOOGLE_API_KEY env var.

vulnerabilities.description: Hardcoded Alpha Vantage API key. Live market data credential. Used for all stock price/fundamental/indicator data fetching.
vulnerabilities.impact: Unauthorized Alpha Vantage API access. Attacker consumes daily request quotas, disrupting all data ingestion.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Rotate via Alpha Vantage. Use ALPHA_VANTAGE_API_KEY env variable.

vulnerabilities.description: Hardcoded MySQL database credentials. Full connection string with username and password in plaintext.
vulnerabilities.impact: Complete database compromise. Attacker with network access can read, modify, or delete all financial data, ML model outputs, and pipeline results.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Change DB password immediately. Build connection URL from environment variables at runtime.

vulnerabilities.description: Live API keys in external submodule .env file: FINANCIAL_DATASETS_API_KEY, ALPHA_VANTAGE_API_KEY, GOOGLE_API_KEY, DEEPSEEK_API_KEY — all with real working values.
vulnerabilities.impact: Four separate API services compromised if this file is committed or exposed. Submodule .env files are outside main repo .gitignore scope.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all real values from external .env files. Create .env.example with placeholders only. Verify each submodule has .env in its own .gitignore.

vulnerabilities.description: Insecure deserialization via joblib.load(). joblib uses pickle internally — malicious .pkl file enables arbitrary code execution (RCE). No file origin verification, signature check, or hash validation.
vulnerabilities.impact: Remote Code Execution if attacker places malicious .pkl file in models/ directory. Complete system compromise possible.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Compute SHA-256 hash of expected model files. Verify hash before loading. Migrate to safer serialization (ONNX, safetensors, or JSON for metadata-only artifacts).

vulnerabilities.description: Second instance of insecure deserialization: joblib.load() in _build_selection_summary(). Same unvalidated pickle RCE vector.
vulnerabilities.impact: Same RCE risk as SEC-006. Both code paths load .pkl files from disk without validation.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply same hash verification and format migration as SEC-006.

vulnerabilities.description: Stored XSS risk: f-string interpolates database values (stock name, exchange, sector, industry) directly into HTML via st.markdown(..., unsafe_allow_html=True). Dynamic DB-origin strings rendered without HTML escaping.
vulnerabilities.impact: Stored XSS — if malicious data enters database via ingestion pipeline (e.g., company name containing <script>), JavaScript executes in all dashboard user sessions.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Use st.write() or st.markdown() without unsafe_allow_html. If HTML formatting required, use html.escape() on all dynamic values before interpolation.

vulnerabilities.description: API keys exported to os.environ at module import time. Three injection sites: pipeline.py:62, fast_evaluators.py:89, deep_evaluators.py:71. Keys become globally readable by any imported module or spawned subprocess.
vulnerabilities.impact: Secrets exposed to entire Python process and child processes. Third-party libraries, logging handlers, or error reporters can accidentally capture and exfiltrate credentials.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Pass credentials via explicit context/dict parameter to backends. Scope environment variables to subprocess.Popen(env={...}) only when spawning external processes.

vulnerabilities.description: Missing authentication on Streamlit dashboard. No login, session management, CSRF protection, or access control. Anyone with network access can view financial data, trigger ML pipeline runs incurring LLM API costs.
vulnerabilities.impact: Unauthorized access to financial analytics platform. Attacker can trigger expensive LLM API calls, corrupt data, or exfiltrate ML model outputs and trading signals.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Add Streamlit-Authenticator or OAuth2 proxy (nginx + oauth2-proxy). Implement session timeout and CSRF protection.

vulnerabilities.description: MySQL connection has no SSL/TLS parameters. Database credentials and all financial data transmitted in cleartext over network.
vulnerabilities.impact: Man-in-the-middle attacker on same network can intercept database traffic including credentials and financial data.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Add SSL parameters to connection URL or set connect_args={'ssl': {'ssl_mode': 'REQUIRED'}} in create_engine() in src/database.py.

vulnerabilities.description: Non-functional credential UI: Alpaca API Key/Secret inputs with type='password' accept values but 'Save API Settings' button performs no storage, validation, or encryption. Creates false sense of security.
vulnerabilities.impact: Users may believe credentials are securely stored when they are discarded. Encourages credential re-entry increasing exposure risk.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Implement actual secure credential storage (keyring library or Streamlit secrets). Or remove non-functional UI until storage is implemented.

vulnerabilities.description: Job error messages from database (error_message column) displayed directly in UI without sanitization. Stack traces or internal paths could leak implementation details.
vulnerabilities.impact: Information disclosure — attacker can learn internal file paths, library versions, or query structures from database error messages.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Sanitize error messages before UI display. Show user-friendly messages, log full details server-side only.

vulnerabilities.description: reports/ directory not in .gitignore. Pipeline output files in reports/full_pipeline/ may contain inline config dumps with API keys and financial data.
vulnerabilities.impact: Accidental commit of pipeline reports containing sensitive financial data, possibly with credentials in inline configuration dumps.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Add reports/ to .gitignore. Verify no report files exist in git history: git log --all -- reports/

vulnerabilities.description: Alpha Vantage API key passed as URL query parameter (params['apikey']). Exposed in HTTP request logs, proxy logs, and packet capture.
vulnerabilities.impact: Low — internal server-to-server calls, but key appears in any HTTP-level logging or packet capture.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Use HTTP header (X-API-Key) instead of query parameter if API supports it. Ensure request logging redacts query strings.

vulnerabilities.description: XSS pattern risk: metric card f-strings with unsafe_allow_html=True (lines 65, 68, 71, 74). Currently numeric values only — low immediate risk but dangerous pattern easily copy-pasted to user-input contexts.
vulnerabilities.impact: Pattern normalization risk — developers may apply same pattern to unsanitized user-controlled strings elsewhere.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Replace metric cards with st.metric() native component. If HTML required, always apply html.escape() to dynamic values.

vulnerabilities.description: Static CSS injected via st.markdown(..., unsafe_allow_html=True). No dynamic content — currently safe but normalizes the unsafe_allow_html pattern throughout codebase.
vulnerabilities.impact: Low — precedent risk. Developers see this pattern at top of main app file and replicate it with dynamic content.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Use Streamlit's native st.html() or inject CSS via a dedicated helper function that rejects dynamic interpolation.

sql_injection_assessment.details: All 12 SQL queries in repository.py use SQLAlchemy 2.0 parameterized queries via text() with named :params or ORM filter methods. Zero instances of string concatenation, f-string interpolation, or format() in SQL statements.

recommendations: IMMEDIATE: Rotate all 3 hardcoded API keys (DeepSeek, Google, Alpha Vantage). These are LIVE credentials in config.yaml and external/ai-hedge-fund/.env.
IMMEDIATE: Change database password from default. Build connection URL from DATABASE_URL environment variable.
IMMEDIATE: Run `git log --all --full-history -- config.yaml` to verify config was never committed. If found in history, purge with git filter-repo.
IMMEDIATE: Strip real API key values from external/ai-hedge-fund/.env and external/TradingAgents/.env. Replace with .env.example template files.
HIGH: Replace all st.markdown(f'...{var}...', unsafe_allow_html=True) with safe Streamlit components. Use html.escape() if raw HTML is unavoidable.
HIGH: Add SHA-256 hash verification before all joblib.load() calls. Migrate model serialization to safetensors or ONNX format.
HIGH: Replace os.environ credential injection (3 sites) with explicit parameter passing or subprocess env= scoping.
MEDIUM: Add Streamlit authentication (OAuth2 proxy, basic auth, or Streamlit-Authenticator).
MEDIUM: Add MySQL SSL/TLS. Set connect_args={'ssl': {'ssl_mode': 'REQUIRED'}} in database.py create_engine().
MEDIUM: Sanitize database error messages before UI display. Log full details server-side only.
MEDIUM: Implement credential persistence in settings page or remove non-functional UI.
LOW: Add reports/ to .gitignore to prevent accidental data exposure.
LOW: Move Alpha Vantage API key from URL query parameter to HTTP header if supported.
LOW: Replace static CSS unsafe_allow_html pattern with st.html() or dedicated CSS helper.
PROCESS: Add `bandit -r src/` to CI/CD pipeline for automated Python security scanning.
PROCESS: Create .env.example with template values; document all required environment variables in README.
PROCESS: Install pre-commit hook (detect-secrets or git-secrets) to block future credential leaks.
PROCESS: Implement quarterly API key rotation policy with documented runbook.
